1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1 — install the toolset with scoop (CLI) +
3	# winget (GUI). Idempotent: only missing packages are installed. This script is
4	# the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed; re-runs whenever the
5	# rendered content (i.e. any toggle below) changes.
6	#
7	# Toggles (from .chezmoi.toml.tmpl):
8	#   installCodingAgents  = {{ .installCodingAgents }}
9	#   installWindowsApps   = {{ .installWindowsApps }}
10	#   installUtilityApps   = {{ .installUtilityApps }}
11	#   installGamingApps    = {{ .installGamingApps }}
12	#   installExtraRuntimes = {{ .installExtraRuntimes }}
13	#   installMediaTools    = {{ .installMediaTools }}
14	#   installLlmTools      = {{ .installLlmTools }}
15	#   installTunnelTools   = {{ .installTunnelTools }}
16	#   installIacTools      = {{ .installIacTools }}
17	#   installHerdr         = {{ .installHerdr }}
18	#   installClink         = {{ .installClink }}
19	#   installTry           = {{ .installTry }}
20	#   useChineseMirror     = {{ .useChineseMirror }}
21	#   managedMachine       = {{ .managedMachine }}
22	
23	# Resilient by design: individual package failures are collected and reported at
24	# the end, never fatal — one bad manifest must not abort the rest of the install
25	# (nor the rest of `chezmoi apply`). Mirrors ansible allow-partial-failure.
26	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
27	if (Test-Path variable:PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference) { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false }
28	$script:Failures = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
29	
30	# UTF-8 console so winget's localized output isn't mojibake on zh-* Windows.
31	try { [Console]::OutputEncoding = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) } catch { $null = $_ }
32	{{ if .useChineseMirror }}
33	# China (GFW) mirrors for install-time package fetches.
34	$env:PIP_INDEX_URL = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
35	$env:UV_DEFAULT_INDEX = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
36	$env:npm_config_registry = 'https://registry.npmmirror.com'
37	$env:GOPROXY = 'https://goproxy.cn,direct'
38	$env:MISE_NODE_MIRROR_URL = 'https://npmmirror.com/mirrors/node/'
39	{{ end }}
40	
41	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
42	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
43	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }
44	
45	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
46	function Test-Admin {
47	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
48	    try {
49	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
50	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
51	    } catch { $false }
52	}
53	
54	function Ensure-Scoop {
55	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
56	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
57	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
58	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
59	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
60	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
61	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
62	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
63	        if (Test-Admin) {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
65	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
66	        } else {
67	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
68	            & $installer
69	        }
70	    }
71	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
72	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
77	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
78	}
79	
80	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
81	    if (-not $apps) { return }
82	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
83	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
84	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
85	    # when only "mise" was missing.
86	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
87	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
88	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
89	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
90	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
91	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
92	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
93	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
94	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
95	    }
96	}
97	
98	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
99	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
100	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
101	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
102	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
103	    Info "winget install $id"
104	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
105	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
106	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
107	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
108	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
109	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
110	    }
111	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
112	}
113	
114	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
115	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. Raycast and the
116	# official ChatGPT desktop app. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
117	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
118	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
119	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
120	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
121	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
122	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `
123	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
124	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id" }
125	}
126	
127	# --- npm CLI agents --------------------------------------------------------
128	function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
129	    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
130	    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
131	        Info "npm i -g $p"
132	        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
133	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
134	    }
135	}

137	# --- herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta) --------------
138	# No scoop/winget/npm manifest — installs via herdr.dev's irm|iex bootstrap
139	# (same shape as Ensure-Scoop). Windows builds are preview-only; the bin junction
140	# is %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Herdr\bin (added to PATH in profile.d/00_env.ps1).
141	function Install-Herdr {
142	    $herdrBin = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Programs\Herdr\bin\herdr.exe'
143	    if ((Have herdr) -or (Test-Path -LiteralPath $herdrBin)) { Info 'herdr already installed'; return }
144	    Info 'installing herdr (preview) via herdr.dev/install.ps1'
145	    try {

153	    }
154	}

164	    'uv', 'gnupg',
165	    # Runtimes are NATIVE via scoop, not mise: mise's activate/shims are

167	    # here; Python via uv (below); go/rust/ruby under Extra runtimes.
168	    # copilot-proxy needs bun/bunx.
169	    'nodejs-lts', 'bun',
170	    # LazyVim pins nvim-treesitter to the `main` branch, which compiles parsers
171	    # by shelling out to `tree-sitter build`. That CLI compiles via Rust's `cc`
172	    # crate, which only knows MSVC / GCC / Clang -- NOT zig (only the retired
173	    # `master` branch accepted zig). So the parser compiler must be a real gcc on
174	    # PATH; zig here does nothing. scoop's `gcc` is nuwen MinGW-w64, amd64-only --
175	    # which also matches the amd64 nvim (native on x64, x64-emulated on ARM64), so
176	    # this ONE line covers both arches (previously gcc was ARM64-only). Plus the
177	    # tree-sitter CLI `main` requires, and win32yank so `unnamedplus` yanks reach
178	    # the Windows clipboard. (Leaner gcc alt: scoop `mingw` = niXman MinGW-w64 UCRT.)
179	    'gcc', 'tree-sitter', 'win32yank'
180	)

182	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
183	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
184	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
185	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
186	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
187	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
188	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
189	if (Have gh) {
190	    gh auth status *> $null
191	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
192	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
193	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {

211	if (Have uv) {

375	# the DOS prompt too (scoop main bucket). pwsh stays the default shell; this is an
376	# opt-in secondary target with prompt + navigation parity only. Our starship.lua
377	# deploys via chezmoi (AppData/Local/clink/starship.lua). The zoxide / fzf Clink
378	# bridges have no scoop/winget manifest (and zoxide has no native cmd target), so

381	Scoop-Install @('clink')

384	    $clinkDir = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'clink'
385	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $clinkDir | Out-Null
386	    $clinkPlugins = @{

389	    }

394	    }
395	    # fzf.default_bindings is a setting DEFINED INSIDE fzf.lua, and `clink set`
396	    # reloads the profile scripts first -- so this must run AFTER fzf.lua is
397	    # downloaded above, else the setting is unknown and the value is silently
398	    # not persisted (the *> $null swallows the "unknown setting" error).
399	    clink set fzf.default_bindings true *> $null    # Ctrl-R history / Ctrl-T files / Alt-C dir
400	} else { Register-Failure 'clink (autorun/plugins not configured)' }
401	{{ end -}}

9	[data]

32	    installHerdr         = {{ promptBoolOnce . "installHerdr" "Install herdr (native Windows terminal multiplexer, preview beta)" false }}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-13 10:02:09Z)**_

我盤點完 `run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` 裡所有安裝項目、加上 WSL/SSH 那幾個獨立腳本，按開關分組估算。**注意：這是用各套件在 Windows 上的典型體積估的（我在 macOS 上無法真的跑 scoop/winget 量測），實際值會浮動；也不含執行期才長大的東西**（scoop cache、Docker image、Ollama model、WSL 磁碟）。

## 各開關的安裝空間估算

| 分組 | 主要吃空間的項目 | 估計（安裝後） |
|---|---|---|
| **Baseline（一定裝）** | gcc/MinGW 工具鏈 ~1–1.3 GB、node+bun ~200 MB、uv+Python ~200 MB、neovim/yazi + 一堆小 CLI | **~2 GB** |
| `installCodingAgents` | Claude Code (winget) + 3 個 npm agent (opencode/codex/copilot) + opencode-desktop + apprise | **~0.7 GB** |
| `installWindowsApps` | **Docker Desktop ~2.5 GB**、Cursor/Antigravity 各 ~0.6 GB、VSCode/PowerToys 各 ~0.35 GB、4 個瀏覽器、Claude/ChatGPT desktop、Hack Nerd Fonts ~0.4 GB | **~7 GB** |
| `installWsl` | WSL2 平台 + kernel（不含 distro） | **~1.5 GB** |
| `installWslUbuntu` | Ubuntu rootfs 展開 + 內部 dotfiles bootstrap | **~2.5 GB** |
| `installUtilityApps` | OBS ~0.45 GB、VLC ~0.2 GB、ShareX、Tailscale、CPU-Z/GPU-Z/HWiNFO… | **~1 GB** |
| `installExtraRuntimes` | **rust/rustup ~1.5 GB**、go ~0.5 GB、ruby ~0.2 GB | **~2.2 GB** |
| `installMediaTools` | ffmpeg + imagemagick | **~0.3 GB** |
| `installLlmTools` | **Ollama ~1.5 GB**（不含模型）+ LiteLLM | **~1.8 GB** |
| `installTunnelTools` | ngrok + cloudflared | **~0.1 GB** |
| `installIacTools` | **Azure CLI ~1 GB** + terraform + opentofu | **~1.5 GB** |
| `installGamingApps` | Steam 客戶端（不含遊戲） | **~0.4 GB** |
| `installSshServer` | OpenSSH server（Windows optional feature） | **~0.01 GB** |
| `installHerdr` | herdr preview | **~0.03 GB** |
| `installClink` | clink + lua 外掛 | **~0.02 GB** |
| `installTry` | ruby（若 ExtraRuntimes 已裝則幾乎 0）+ try-cli gem | **~0.2 GB / ~0** |

## 兩個常見情境的合計

**預設 `workstation` role**（`$full` 打開的是 CodingAgents + WindowsApps + WSL + UtilityApps + ExtraRuntimes，其餘 false）：

```
Baseline 2.0 + Agents 0.7 + WindowsApps 7.0 + WSL 1.5 + Utility 1.0 + ExtraRuntimes 2.2
≈ 14–15 GB
```

**全部開關都打開**：再加 WslUbuntu 2.5 + Iac 1.5 + Llm 1.8 + Media 0.3 + Gaming 0.4 + Tunnel/SSH/Herdr/Clink/Try ≈ **~21–25 GB**。

`minimal` role（只有 baseline）≈ **~2 GB**。

## 要注意的幾點

- **四個「無底洞」不含在上表**，實際硬碟消耗會遠超估計：Docker Desktop 的 image/WSL VM 磁碟、Ollama 下載的模型（動輒每個 4–70 GB）、WSL Ubuntu distro 用越久越大、以及 scoop 的下載 cache（約再多一份壓縮檔，可用 `scoop cache rm *` 清）。
- **最大的單一項目**排序：Docker Desktop、rust toolchain、Ollama、gcc/MinGW、Azure CLI、Cursor/Antigravity。若要瘦身，關掉 `installWindowsApps`（省 ~7 GB）或 `installLlmTools`/`installIacTools` 效果最明顯。
- gcc 那條（LazyVim 的 tree-sitter 需要真 gcc 編 parser）是 baseline 裡最重的，佔 baseline 一半，但它是強制的。

要不要我把這份估算整理成 `docs/rationale.md` 或 `docs/tools.md` 裡的一個「磁碟空間預算」小節（含 zh-TW 雙語）？那樣才會進版控。

result: 全裝約 20–25 GB（預設 workstation ~14–15 GB、minimal ~2 GB），已按 16 個開關逐項估算成表；不含 Docker image / Ollama 模型 / WSL 磁碟 / scoop cache 等執行期才長大的資料。

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/tools.md`

```text
1	# Tools
2	
3	The install set lives in `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
4	(the single source of truth), gated by the init toggles. **scoop** handles CLI
5	tools; **winget** handles GUI apps.
6	
7	## CLI tools (scoop)
8	
9	| Tool | Purpose |
10	|---|---|
11	| git, gh, glab | version control + GitHub / GitLab CLIs |
12	| neovim | editor (LazyVim, pre-configured) |
13	| lazygit | git TUI (delta pager configured) |
14	| zoxide | smarter `cd` |
15	| fzf, fd, ripgrep | fuzzy find / file search / grep |
16	| bat, eza | `cat` / `ls` replacements |
17	| delta | git diff pager |
18	| jq | JSON processor |
19	| yazi, btop | file manager / system monitor |
20	| television (`tv`) | fuzzy picker / channel launcher |
21	| tldr (tlrc) | community man-page cheatsheets; `tldrf` adds `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
22	| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (gh extension; installs once `gh` is authenticated) |
23	| starship | prompt |
24	| node | JS runtime (`nodejs-lts`) |
25	| uv | Python package/runtime manager |
26	| bun | JS runtime + package manager (backs copilot-proxy) |
27	| just | task runner (this repo's `justfile`) |
28	| make | GNU make |
29	| gcc | C compiler for Neovim tree-sitter parsers (MinGW-w64; nvim-treesitter's `main` branch compiles via `tree-sitter build` → Rust `cc` crate, which needs gcc/clang/MSVC — **not** zig) |
30	| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI (nvim-treesitter `main` branch) |
31	| win32yank | clipboard provider for Neovim |
32	| gnupg | `gpg` (git commit signing, verification) |
33	
34	Runtimes are **native (scoop), not mise** — see [rationale](rationale.md#runtimes-scoop-natives-not-mise-on-windows).
35	`node` (`nodejs-lts`) and `bun` come from scoop; `go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`
36	are added when **Extra runtimes** is enabled. A default **Python** is uv-managed:
37	`uv python install --default --preview` puts `python`/`python3` in `~/.local/bin`
38	(on PATH ahead of the Store's `python.exe` app-alias), so `python`, `uv run`, and
39	`uv venv` all work without the Microsoft Store.
40	
41	## GUI apps (winget)
42	
43	| App | winget id |
44	|---|---|
45	| VSCode | `Microsoft.VisualStudioCode` |
46	| Cursor | `Anysphere.Cursor` |
47	| Windows Terminal | `Microsoft.WindowsTerminal` |
48	| Alacritty | `Alacritty.Alacritty` |
49	| WezTerm | `wez.wezterm` (tmux-like multiplexer; managed `~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua` sets pwsh as the shell + Nerd Font) |
50	| PowerToys | `Microsoft.PowerToys` |
51	| Raycast | `9PFXXSHC64H3` (msstore) |
52	| Antigravity | `Google.Antigravity` |
53	| Docker Desktop | `Docker.DockerDesktop` (WSL2 backend via the `installWsl` toggle — see below) |
54	| Discord | `Discord.Discord` |
55	| Claude Desktop | `Anthropic.Claude` |
56	| ChatGPT | `9PLM9XGG6VKS` (msstore) |
57	| Chrome | `Google.Chrome` |
58	| Arc | `TheBrowserCompany.Arc` |
59	| Zen Browser | `Zen-Team.Zen-Browser` |
60	| Grammarly | `Grammarly.Grammarly` |
61	| Steam (gaming) | `Valve.Steam` |
62	
63	**Docker Desktop's WSL2 backend** (`installWsl` toggle, on for workstation) is set up
64	by `scripts/enable-wsl.ps1` via `wsl --install --no-distribution` — only the WSL2
65	platform, not a Linux distro (Docker creates its own `docker-desktop` distro).
66	`wsl --install` needs admin **and** a reboot, so a non-elevated `chezmoi apply`
67	self-elevates with a single UAC prompt (like scoop), runs the install, then prints a
68	restart notice — reboot, then Docker Desktop starts on the WSL2 backend. Re-run or
69	retry (e.g. if the UAC prompt was declined) with `just enable-wsl`. Already-installed
70	machines are a no-op (no UAC). On a proxy / corporate / GFW network where the WSL app
71	download is reset ("connection reset"), the script falls back to enabling the WSL2
72	features **offline via DISM** and points at the kernel MSI + `wsl --update`. WSL as a
73	*Linux shell* stays out of scope — see
74	[rationale](rationale.md#powershell-7-default-cmdexe-optional-via-clink).
75	
76	**Unattended WSL Ubuntu + dotfiles** (`installWslUbuntu`, off by default) goes further:
77	`scripts/enable-wsl-ubuntu.ps1` registers `Ubuntu-24.04` with **no OOBE** (creates your
78	user via `wsl -u root`, passwordless sudo, WSL auto-login), then — in the default
79	`headless` mode — runs a **frozen-from-Windows** chezmoi one-liner that installs the
80	cross-platform dotfiles (`daviddwlee84/dotfiles`, `ubuntu_server` profile) with no
81	prompts inside WSL. `wslUbuntuBootstrap` can instead be `interactive` (the parent repo's
82	prompts on first login) or `none` (set up the distro only). Requires the WSL2 platform
83	(`installWsl`) + its reboot first; run or retry with `just enable-wsl-ubuntu`. Linux GUI
84	apps work out of the box via **WSLg** if you later switch the frozen profile to
85	`ubuntu_desktop`. This is an opt-in *bridge* — the Linux config lives in the
86	cross-platform repo, not here.
87	
88	## Utility apps (winget)
89	
90	Enabled by **Install utility apps**:
91	
92	| App | winget id | What |
93	|---|---|---|
94	| CPU-Z | `CPUID.CPU-Z` | CPU/mainboard info |
95	| GPU-Z | `TechPowerUp.GPU-Z` | GPU info + sensors |
96	| HWiNFO | `REALiX.HWiNFO` | full hardware monitoring |
97	| TreeSize Free | `JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free` | disk-usage explorer |
98	| WinDirStat | `WinDirStat.WinDirStat` | disk-usage treemap |
99	| Everything | `voidtools.Everything` | instant filename search |
100	| ShareX | `ShareX.ShareX` | screenshots + screen recording |
101	| OBS Studio | `OBSProject.OBSStudio` | screen recording / streaming |
102	| VLC | `VideoLAN.VLC` | media player |
103	| Rufus | `Rufus.Rufus` | bootable USB creator |
104	| Tailscale | `Tailscale.Tailscale` | mesh VPN |
105	
106	!!! note "Launchers & the Alt+Space clash"
107	    **Raycast** (Microsoft Store, id `9PFXXSHC64H3`) is the default launcher.
108	    It shares **Alt+Space** with **PowerToys Run** — disable PowerToys Run
109	    (PowerToys Settings › PowerToys Run › off) or rebind one. Raycast for Windows
110	    is still a Store beta; if it won't install, fall back to
111	    [Flow Launcher](https://www.flowlauncher.com/):
112	    `winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher`.
113	
114	!!! note "Notepad++ is opt-in"
115	    Dropped from the default set: on Windows-on-ARM its installer is a
116	    low-reputation binary that Windows Defender / SmartScreen flags as PUA
117	    (a false positive, aggravated by managed-machine PUA protection), and it's
118	    redundant with VSCode / Cursor / nvim. Add it yourself if you want it:
119	    `winget install Notepad++.Notepad++` or `scoop install notepadplusplus`.
120	
121	!!! note "Managed / corporate machines"
122	    The **Managed machine** init toggle skips apps that org policy commonly
123	    blocks — **Grammarly** and **Tailscale** (its MSI fails with exit `1625`
124	    under policy). Leave the toggle off on a personal machine to install them.
125	
126	!!! note "Alacritty & Nerd Fonts (machine-wide install)"
127	    scoop's `nerd-fonts` bucket registers **Hack Nerd Font Mono** *per-user*
128	    (HKCU). Windows Terminal and WezTerm see per-user fonts, but **Alacritty on
129	    Windows reads only the machine-wide font collection**, so it can't find the
130	    font and falls back to a default. Fix (needs admin):
131	    `just install-fonts-machine-wide` (runs `scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono Hack-NF`),
132	    then restart Alacritty. Diagnose the family name with
133	    `[System.Drawing.FontFamily]::Families.Name | Select-String Hack`.
134	
135	## AI agents
136	
137	Enabled by **Install coding agents**:
138	
139	- **Claude Code** — `Anthropic.ClaudeCode` via **winget** (the native Windows
140	  build; distinct from `Anthropic.Claude`, the desktop app under GUI apps).
141	  Upgraded with `just upgrade-winget`, not npm.
142	- **OpenCode** (`opencode-ai`), **Codex** (`@openai/codex`), **Copilot CLI**
143	  (`@github/copilot`) — installed globally via **npm** (provided by the scoop `node`).
144	- **OpenCode Desktop** — GUI companion to the `opencode-ai` CLI, via scoop
145	  (`extras/opencode-desktop`).
146	- **claude-hud** — a statusline HUD that renders below the Claude Code prompt.
147	  Enabled in `~/.claude/settings.json` by a `run_onchange` merger
148	  (`.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_25_claude_settings.ps1.tmpl`) that
149	  additively deep-merges an overlay, preserving your live settings. The plugin
150	  self-installs from its marketplace on first launch and runs on the scoop
151	  `bun`/`node` runtime; the merger also enables the `pyright-lsp` plugin and sets
152	  `permissions.defaultMode: auto` + `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`. **Restart
153	  Claude Code** after `chezmoi apply` to see the HUD. See
154	  [running multiple agents](claude-code-agents.md).
155	- **Desktop notifications** — `apprise` (`uv tool install --with pywin32 apprise`)
156	  fires native Windows toasts on Claude Code's **Notification** (needs your
157	  attention) and **Stop** (task finished) events, via a native-pwsh hook
158	  (`~/.claude/hooks/notify.ps1`) the settings overlay wires into `hooks`. Routing
159	  lives in `~/.config/apprise/apprise.yaml` — the `windows://` backend tagged
160	  `desktop`; add your own remote services (Discord/Slack/…) in the create-once
161	  `~/.config/apprise/custom.yaml`. Port of the parent repo's `notify.sh` +
162	  `apprise.yaml`.
163	
164	The official **ChatGPT** desktop app installs via the Microsoft Store (see GUI
165	apps); **Codex** is the `@openai/codex` npm CLI above, not a Store app. SpecStory
166	has no native-Windows CLI package, so it's omitted here.
167	
168	## PowerShell modules (PSGallery)
169	
170	- **PSFzf** — fzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history).
171	- **AudioDeviceCmdlets** — absolute volume + mute for the `sysvol`/`sysmute` helpers.
172	
173	## Opt-in dev stacks
174	
175	Off by default; enable the matching init prompt:
176	
177	| Toggle | Installs |
178	|---|---|
179	| Local LLM tools | Ollama (`Ollama.Ollama`) + LiteLLM (`uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'`) |
180	| Tunnel tools | ngrok, cloudflared (scoop) |
181	| IaC tools | Terraform, OpenTofu (scoop) + Azure CLI & the `azure-devops` extension (`az devops`/`az repos`, winget) |
182	| OpenSSH server | Microsoft OpenSSH Server (sshd): Windows capability + auto-start service + inbound TCP 22 firewall rule, with pwsh as the default shell. Needs admin — set up on an elevated `chezmoi apply`, or run `just enable-sshd` from an elevated pwsh. |
183	| herdr multiplexer | herdr, a native Windows terminal multiplexer (**preview beta**). No scoop/winget manifest — installs via herdr.dev's `irm \| iex` script; config managed at `~/.config/herdr/config.toml` (pwsh as the default shell). See [rationale](rationale.md#terminal-multiplexer-wezterm-stable-default-herdr-native-beta). |
184	| Clink (cmd.exe) | [Clink](https://chrisant996.github.io/clink/) (scoop `main`) — Bash-style line editing for `cmd.exe`, so **starship** + **zoxide** + **fzf** work in the DOS prompt. Reuses the shared `starship.toml`; registers a per-user cmd AutoRun, deploys our `starship.lua`, and fetches the community `clink-zoxide` / `clink-fzf` bridges into `%LocalAppData%\clink`. pwsh stays the default — this is an opt-in secondary shell with prompt + nav parity only. See [rationale](rationale.md#powershell-7-default-cmdexe-optional-via-clink) & [Shell](shell.md#cmdexe-via-clink). |
185	| try (ephemeral workspaces) | [`try`](https://github.com/tobi/try) via `gem install try-cli` (ruby installed if absent). Dated `~/src/tries/YYYY-MM-DD-name` trial dirs + a fuzzy selector; `tri <git-url>` clones into one. The pwsh command is **`tri`** (`try` is a reserved keyword — bareword `try` won't parse; `& try` works). See [Shell](shell.md#try). |
186	
187	**China mirrors** routes pip / npm / cargo / go / node package fetches through GFW
188	mirrors (Tsinghua / npmmirror / goproxy.cn / rsproxy), applied both at install time
189	and in the interactive shell (`profile.d/05_mirrors.ps1`).
190	
191	## Television (tv) channels
192	
193	Custom pickers under `%APPDATA%\television\cable\` (run `tv` to browse, or `tv <name>`):
194	
195	| Channel | What |
196	|---|---|
197	| `aliases` | PowerShell aliases & functions (cached at shell startup) |
198	| `git-ops` | common git commands → Enter copies to clipboard |
199	| `ports` | listening TCP ports → Ctrl+K kills the owning process |
200	| `kill-process` | processes by memory → Enter/Ctrl+K kill, Ctrl+D force-kill |
201	| `scoop-apps` | installed scoop apps → Enter info, Ctrl+U update, Ctrl+X uninstall |
202	| `apps` | launch an app from Start Menu shortcuts |
203	| `channels` | browse every tv channel by description → Enter opens the selected one |
204	
205	## Upgrades
206	
207	Install and upgrade are separate. `chezmoi apply` only installs what's missing;
208	upgrades are explicit:
209	
210	```powershell
211	just upgrade-scoop     # scoop update *
212	just upgrade-winget    # winget upgrade --all
213	```
214
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# 工具索引
2	
3	安裝清單在 `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
4	（單一事實來源），並由初始化開關控制。**scoop** 負責 CLI 工具；**winget** 負責

6	
7	## CLI 工具（scoop）
8	
9	| 工具 | 用途 |
10	|---|---|
11	| git、gh、glab | 版本控制 + GitHub / GitLab CLI |
12	| neovim | 編輯器（已配置 LazyVim） |
13	| lazygit | git TUI（已配置 delta 分頁器） |
14	| zoxide | 更聰明的 `cd` |
15	| fzf、fd、ripgrep | 模糊搜尋 / 找檔案 / grep |
16	| bat、eza | `cat` / `ls` 替代品 |
17	| delta | git diff 分頁器 |
18	| jq | JSON 處理器 |
19	| yazi、btop | 檔案管理員 / 系統監控 |

21	| tldr（tlrc） | 社群 man page 速查表；`tldrf` 加上 `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
22	| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue 儀表板 TUI（gh 擴充；`gh` 登入後才會安裝） |
23	| starship | 提示字元 |
24	| node | JS runtime（`nodejs-lts`） |
25	| uv | Python 套件/runtime 管理器 |
26	| bun | JS runtime + 套件管理器（copilot-proxy 用） |
27	| just | 任務執行器（本 repo 的 `justfile`） |
28	| make | GNU make |
29	| gcc | Neovim tree-sitter parser 的 C 編譯器（MinGW-w64；nvim-treesitter `main` 分支透過 `tree-sitter build` → Rust `cc` crate 編譯，需要 gcc/clang/MSVC —— **不是** zig） |
30	| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI（nvim-treesitter `main` 分支） |
31	| win32yank | Neovim 的剪貼簿提供者 |
32	| gnupg | `gpg`（git commit 簽章、驗證） |
33	
34	Runtime **改用原生（scoop），不用 mise** —— 見 [rationale](rationale.md)。`node`
35	（`nodejs-lts`）與 `bun` 由 scoop 安裝；啟用 **Extra runtimes** 時才會加裝 `go`、
36	`rust`（rustup）、`ruby`。預設 **Python** 由 uv 管理：`uv python install --default --preview`
37	會把 `python`/`python3` 放到 `~/.local/bin`（PATH 上排在 Store 的 `python.exe`

 `./docs/index.md`

```text
1	# Windows dotfiles
2	
3	Native **Windows + PowerShell 7** dotfiles, managed by [chezmoi](https://chezmoi.io).
4	A self-contained, Windows-only companion to the cross-platform (macOS/Linux)
5	dotfiles at [daviddwlee84/dotfiles](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles) — the
6	PowerShell layer is written natively rather than ported line-by-line from the POSIX
7	shell config.
8	
9	!!! tip "Which repo do I install from?"
10	    - **Native Windows (PowerShell)** → this repo (`dotfiles-windows`).
11	    - **macOS / Linux / WSL** → [daviddwlee84/dotfiles](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles).
12	
13	## What you get
14	
15	- **Shell**: PowerShell 7 with a modular `$PROFILE` that dot-sources
16	  `~/.config/powershell/profile.d/*.ps1`.
17	- **Prompt**: [starship](https://starship.rs) — the same `starship.toml` used on
18	  macOS/Linux.
19	- **CLI tools** via [scoop](https://scoop.sh): git, neovim, lazygit, zoxide, fzf,
20	  bat, eza, ripgrep, fd, gh, delta, jq, yazi, btop, mise, uv, node, bun.
21	- **AI agents**: Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, GitHub Copilot CLI, SpecStory, Antigravity.
22	- **Editors**: VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++ (shared settings + keybindings).
23	- **Apps** via [winget](https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/package-manager/):
24	  Windows Terminal, Alacritty, Raycast, PowerToys, Steam.
25	- **`copilot-proxy`** tool series, rewritten as a native PowerShell module — see
26	  [copilot-proxy](copilot-proxy.md).
27	
28	## Quick start
29	
30	```powershell
31	irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
32	```
33	
34	Full walkthrough: [Setup](setup.md). Design choices (why scoop, why starship, why
35	pwsh): [Rationale](rationale.md).
36	
37	## How it's organized
38	
39	| Path | What |
40	|---|---|
41	| `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` | Init prompts (role + feature toggles). |
42	| `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` | `$PROFILE` loader. |
43	| `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/` | Modular shell fragments. |
44	| `dot_config/powershell/modules/Copilot/` | `copilot-proxy` module. |
45	| `.chezmoiscripts/` | Package install + editor/terminal overlays. |
46	| `bootstrap.ps1` | One-line installer. |
47	
48	!!! note "Scope"
49	    This repo targets native Windows only. WSL is covered by the separate
50	    cross-platform dotfiles (it's just another Linux host there).
51
```
</details></tool-use>
